name: Pine Nut-Spinach Pesto
recipeInstructions:
You can substitute sunflower kernels or chopped almonds for the pine nuts if desired.
A sprinkle of Parmesan cheese if using this sauce for pasta, or a mixture of yogurt and mayonnaise if using as a sauce for grilled chicken or fish.
PESTO: Place frozen spinach in a microwave-safe dish. Cover with vented plastic wrap and thaw several minutes on high. Break up the center with a fork. Drain the spinach, pressing out excess moisture. Process all ingredients except tomatoes, green onions, and pine nuts in a food processor until smooth. Return to the microwave bowl or transfer to a saucepan if cooking on the stovetop. Cook for 2 minutes or stir over medium heat until thickened. Stir in the chopped tomatoes, green onions, and toasted pine nuts. Toss with pasta and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. For grilled chicken or fish, mix mayonnaise and yogurt with the pesto before serving. Yield: About 1-1/2 cups
